person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Les armes de Courtenay se blasonnent ainsi : 
D'or à trois tourteaux de gueules, appelée besant, pièce de monnaie ronde d'or ou d'argent, frappée autrefois à Byzance (aujourd'hui Istanbul), d'où elle tire son nom. Ancienne monnaie des Empereurs de Byzance, elle évoque les croisades et les voyages d'Orient.

Ces armes sont celles de la 1ère maison de Courtenay, dont plusieurs membres ont été sacrés empereurs latins d’Orient.
La seigneurie de Courtenay a eu pour fondateur au début du 11ème siècle Athon de Château-Renard, sir de Courtenay, descendant de Rainart, comte de Sens. Parmi ses membres, se sont illustrés principalement Jocelin 1er le Grand, seigneur de Tibériade en 1115, puis comte d’Edesse en 1119. Elisabeth qui épousa en 1150 Pierre 1er de France, 7ème fils du roi Louis VI, ce qui fit de la maison de Courtenay une branche de la dynastie royale de France (voir l’arbre généalogique en Mairie). Pierre II qui devint comte de Nevers, d’Auxerre, et de Tonnerre par son 1er mariage et empereur latin d’Orient en 1216 par son 2ème mariage. Robert 1er et Baudouin II qui furent aussi empereurs de Constantinople jusqu’en 1261, puis Catherine 1ère qui resta impératrice titulaire. Elle épousa Charles de Valois, le comté de Courtenay appartint ensuite aux Valois et passa dans le domaine royal.
Source :
Histoire de la maison de Courtenay
relevé JPR Teyssier ChalanconRenaud de Courtenay, (d. 1190) anglicised to Reginald I de Courtenay, of Sutton, Berkshire, was a French nobleman of the House of Courtenay who took up residence in England and founded the English Courtenay family, who became Earls of Devon in 1335. The title is still held today, by his direct male descendant.

Origins
He was the son of Miles (Milo) de Courtenay, Seigneur (lord of the manor) of Courtenay, in the Kingdom of France, today in the Loiret department in north-central France, by his wife Ermengard de Nevers.

Career
He succeeded his father as Seigneur of Courtenay. He fought in the Second Crusade, with King Louis VII of France. He quarrelled with King Louis VII, who seized Renaud's French possessions and gave them along with Renaud's daughter Elizabeth to his youngest brother, Pierre (Peter) of France, who thenceforth became known as Peter I of Courtenay (died 1183)). He became Lord of the Manor of Sutton in 1161.

Marriages
(1) Helene (Hawise) du Donjon, daughter of Frederick du Donjon and Corbeil, sister of Guy du Donjon
(2) Maud du Sap, daughter of Robert FitzEdith, lord of Okehampton (d.1172) (illegitimate son of Henry I).

Progeny
By his first marriage:

(1) Renaud II de Courtenay, (b. 1125 - d. 27 September 1194) who in 1172, accompanied King Henry II of England on the Irish Expedition to the Wexford. He married Hawise de Curcy (d.1219), heiress of the feudal barony of Okehampton in Devon, and half sister to his father's second wife, Maud du Sap. Through the marriage, he acquired Okehampton Castle, which remained in the Courtenay family for many generations. They had a son, Robert de Courtenay (d. 1242), who was the great-grandfather of Hugh de Courtenay, 1st Earl of Devon (d.1340).
(2) Elizabeth de Courtenay (b. 1127 - d. September 1205), who was given in marriage by the French King Louis VII (d.1180) to his youngest brother, Peter of France (d.1183), who thenceforth became known as "Peter I of Courtenay".

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Renaud_de_Courtenay

Gâtinais (Loiret), Bourgogne, Ile-de-France, Angleterre

http://racineshistoire.free.fr/LGN/PDF/Courtenay.pdf

Renaud de Courtenay (Anglicised to Reginald; died 4 September 1164), of Sutton, Berkshire, was a French nobleman of the House of Courtenay who came over to England and founded of the English Courtenay family which became Earls of Devon in 1335, which title is still held today by his direct male descendant. «b»Origins«/b» He was the son of Miles (Milo) de Courtenay, Seigneur (lord of the manor) of Courtenay, in the Kingdom of France, today in the Loiret department in north-central France, by his wife Ermengard de Nevers. «b»Career«/b» He succeeded his father as Seigneur of Courtenay. He fought in the Second Crusade, with King Louis VII of France. He quarrelled with King Louis VII, who seized Renaud's French possessions and gave them along with Renaud's daughter Elizabeth to his youngest brother, Pierre (Peter) of France, who thenceforth became known as Peter I of Courtenay (died 1183)). He became Lord of the Manor of Sutton in 1161. «b»Marriages«/b» 1.) Helene (Hawise) du Donjon, daughter of Frederick du Donjon and Corbeil, sister of Guy du Donjon 2.) Maud du Sap, daughter of Robert FitzEdith, lord of Okehampton (d.1172) (illegitimate son of Henry I). «b»Progeny By his first marriage:«/b» 1.) Renaud II de Courtenay, (b. 1125-d. 27 September 1194) who in 1172, accompanied King Henry II of England on the Irish Expedition to the Wexford. He married Hawise de Curcy (d.1219), heiress of the feudal barony of Okehampton in Devon, and half sister to his father's second wife, Maud du Sap. Through the marriage, he acquired Okehampton Castle, which remained in the Courtenay family for many generations. They had a son, Robert de Courtenay (d. 1242), who was the great-grandfather of Hugh de Courtenay, 1st Earl of Devon (d.1340). 2.) Elizabeth de Courtenay (b. 1127-d. September 1205), who was given in marriage by the French King Louis VII (d.1180) to his youngest brother, Peter of France (d.1183), who thenceforth became known as Peter I of Courtenay (d.1183).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Renaud de Courtenay I於1100年6月10日出生在法國崔-瓦爾 德 洛易洛易特庫尼時，他的父親Milo de Courtenay32歲，母親Ermengard De Nevers11097歲。他於大約1168年與Hawise de Abrincis de Curcy在英國英格蘭戴文奧可哈普頓結婚。他於1145年登記服兵役。他去世於1194年9月27日在英國英格蘭波克夏塞頓 庫尼享年94歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭戴文艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Courtenay near Sens in northern France, or some other place similarly named, from the name of a Gallo-Roman landlord, Curtenus (a derivative of Latin curtus ‘short’) + the locative suffix -acum.
Irish: English surname adopted for Ó Curnáin ‘descendant of Curnán’, an Old Irish personal name based on a diminutive of corn ‘horn’.

History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Athon de Courtenay, who lived in Courtenay in Loiret in the early 11th century. Renaud de Courtenay held land in Devon in the 12th century, and the family later held the title of Earls of Devon following the marriage of Hugh de Courtenay to Margaret de Bohun, granddaughter of King Edward I, in 1325.

nameMeanings.name: Renaud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Renaud de Courtenay I was born on 10 June 1100, in Courtenay, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France, his father, Milo de Courtenay, was 32 and his mother, Ermengard De Nevers, was 11097. He married Hawise de Abrincis de Curcy about 1168, in Okehampton, Devon, England, United Kingdom. He registered for military service in 1145. He died on 27 September 1194, in Sutton Courtenay, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 94, and was buried in Abbey, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Courtenay, 45320, Loiret, France: 프랑스 세트레-발 데 로이르 로이렛 쿠르텐아이
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Renaud de Courtenay I(이)가 1100년 6월 10일 프랑스 세트레-발 데 로이르 로이렛 쿠르텐아이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Milo de Courtenay(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Ermengard De Nevers(은)는 11097세였다. 그는 대략 1168년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 오케함프턴에서 Hawise de Abrincis de Curcy(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1145년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1194년 9월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 수턴 쿠르텐아이에서 94세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.10 juin 1100–27 septembre 1194: 10 de junho de 1100–27 de setembro de 1194
pt.Sutton Courtenay, Berkshire, England: Sutton Courtenay, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.of, Courtenay, Loiret, France: Courtenay, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, França
pt.Courtenay, Loiret, Centre, France: Courtenay, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, França
pt.Courtenay, 45320, Loiret, France: Courtenay, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, França
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Renaud de Courtenay I nasceu em 10 de junho de 1100, em Courtenay, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, França, seu pai, Milo de Courtenay, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Ermengard De Nevers, tinha 11097 anos. Ele casou-se com Hawise de Abrincis de Curcy aproximadamente 1168, em Okehampton, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1145. Ele faleceu em 27 de setembro de 1194, em Sutton Courtenay, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 94 anos, e foi sepultado em Abbey, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.of, Courtenay, Loiret, France: коуртенеи, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция
ru.Courtenay, Loiret, Centre, France: коуртенеи, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция
ru.Courtenay, 45320, Loiret, France: коуртенеи, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Renaud de Courtenay I родился 10 июня 1100 г. в местности коуртенеи, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция, его отцу по имени Milo de Courtenay было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Ermengard De Nevers, 11097. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise de Abrincis de Curcy около 1168 г. в местности окэамптон, девон, Англия, Великобритания. В 1145 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 27 сентября 1194 г. в местности суттон коуртенеи, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 94 лет и был похоронен в местности эбби, девон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.10 juin 1100–27 septembre 1194: 10 de junio de 1100–27 de septiembre de 1194

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Renaud de Courtenay I nació el 10 de junio de 1100, en Courtenay, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, Francia, su papá, Milo de Courtenay, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Ermengard De Nevers, tenía 11097 años. Se casó con Hawise de Abrincis de Curcy aproximadamente 1168, en Okehampton, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1145. Murió el 27 de septiembre de 1194, en Sutton Courtenay, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 94 años, y fue sepultado en Abbey, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
